source_jobs.title: Data Scientist (Product Analytics)
source_jobs.description:
DATA SCIENTIST (PRODUCT ANALYTICS)


EDINBURGH OR LONDON




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

As our product scales across global enterprises, understanding exactly how users interact with our AI agents is critical to our success. This role exists to turn raw usage data into the strategic insights that will shape the future of our product roadmap.

You will sit directly at the intersection of Product, Engineering, and Design, acting as the analytical conscience of the team. This is a high-impact role requiring a blend of deep technical skill and product intuition. You will define what "good" looks like for our platform and build the metrics, models, and experiments to track it.

This isn't a passive reporting or basic data-pull role. It combines advanced product analytics, experimental design, and strategic decision support. Your mission is to uncover the behavioural patterns that drive core adoption, optimize user retention, and ensure our AI agents deliver maximum value to every customer.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Product Analytics & Metrics Framework

 - Instrument Core Analytics: Design, instrument, and validate product analytics—including event tracking, funnels, retention curves, and feature-adoption metrics.

 - Build the Metrics Infrastructure: Build and maintain the core metrics framework and the dashboards that surface them to the broader company.

 - Improve Data Quality: Partner with engineering to improve data instrumentation, event schemas, and tracking quality at the source.

 - Empower Self-Serve Access: Build intuitive self-serve analytics tooling and data models so internal stakeholders can easily answer their own day-to-day questions.

Experimentation & Deep-Dive Analysis

 - Own Statistical Rigour: Design, run, and assess A/B tests and other controlled experiments; own the statistical validity of our testing culture from hypothesis definition to the final decision.

 - Analyze User Behavior: Conduct deep-dive behavioral analyses, cohort performance tracking, and churn driver investigations to uncover critical product friction points.

 - Develop Predictive Models: Build predictive and segmentation models to anticipate user needs, flag churn risk, and identify expansion opportunities.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Translate Ambiguity: Translate ambiguous or open-ended product questions into well-scoped technical analyses and clear, actionable recommendations.

 - Advocate for the User: Act as the voice of user behavior within the GTM and Product teams, feeding builders with empirical insight on how users actually interact with the platform.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Essential

 - 3–5+ years of experience in a dedicated product data science or product analytics role.

 - Strong technical toolkit: Advanced proficiency in SQL and Python/R for data manipulation, statistical analysis, and predictive modeling.

 - Genuinely product-minded: Deeply curious about why users behave the way they do, not just what the top-line numbers say.

 - Rigorous experimentation

source_jobs.title: Account Executive - Enterprise UK&I
source_jobs.description:
ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE - ENTERPRISE UK&I

Edinburgh or London




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring an Enterprise Account Executive to lead complex, high-value deals across large organisations.

You’ll be responsible for building and progressing a focused set of strategic opportunities. This means navigating multiple stakeholders, building internal champions, and creating alignment across legal, technical, and commercial teams.

You’ll work closely with Legal Engineers, Product, and Leadership, but you own the deal strategy and the outcome.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own a portfolio of high-value, enterprise opportunities

 - Build and execute deal strategies across complex buying groups

 - Navigate stakeholders across Legal, Finance, Security, IT, and Procurement

 - Develop strong champions and drive internal alignment within accounts

 - Lead longer sales cycles (6–12+ months) with clear structure and momentum

 - Partner closely with GTM Legal Engineers on solution design

 - Maintain accurate forecasting and clear deal visibility




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience closing complex enterprise SaaS deals

 - Track record of landing and expanding high-value accounts

 - Strong stakeholder management across multiple functions

 - Ability to build champions and navigate internal politics

 - Structured, strategic approach to deal management

 - High ownership and accountability for outcomes




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team. We work together because it makes us faster, sharper, and more effective. You should expect to be in the office our out visiting clients as your default.

This is a high performance environment. We set a high bar, move quickly, and expect people to take ownership of real problems.

There isn’t always a clear path. The people who do well here figure things out, stay close to the work, and keep pushing until they get to the right outcome.

This role requires patience, structure, and strong judgement. You’ll be trusted to run complex deals with a high degree of autonomy.




WHY WORDSMITH

We’re in a period of strong momentum — growing quickly, winning new customers, and expanding into larger accounts.

The product is landing well, the market is ready, and the team is scaling to meet demand.

If you want to be in the room, close meaningful deals, and grow quickly in a high bar environment, this is one of the best places to do it.

source_jobs.title: Motion Graphic Designer
source_jobs.description:
MOTION DESIGNER

London / Edinburgh




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT

Motion is one of the fastest ways to show people what Wordsmith actually does.

A strong product animation can communicate more in 30 seconds than a long case study ever will.

This is a founding motion role. You’ll define how Wordsmith moves, feels, and tells product stories through video. You’ll build the systems that let us scale high-quality output and shape how the market experiences our brand.

If you care about craft, speed, and work that directly drives growth, this is a rare opportunity.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Motion Designer to own video and motion across Wordsmith.

You’ll create launch videos, feature announcements, social assets, and product storytelling that turns complex workflows into clear, compelling narratives.

You’ll work closely with Design, Growth, Product Marketing, and Founders to bring ideas to life quickly and at a high standard.

This is a hands-on role with real ownership from day one.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Create launch videos, feature announcements, and product animations

 - Turn abstract workflows into clear visual stories that land quickly

 - Build a motion system of templates, transitions, components, and export standards

 - Produce social content, event openers, conference loops, and sales enablement assets

 - Partner with writers, designers, and producers from concept to final delivery

 - Brief and manage freelancers when extra capacity is needed

 - Help define how motion supports brand, growth, and product storytelling

 - Use AI tools to improve quality, speed, and creative range




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years of motion design experience, ideally in-house at a product or technology company

 - Strong reel of shipped work you can talk through clearly

 - Excellent taste and high standards for craft

 - Strong narrative instincts — you can simplify dense ideas visually

 - Ownership mindset — you’re comfortable building a function, not just executing briefs

 - High initiative — you spot gaps and move before being asked

 - AI-first mentality — you actively use modern tools to increase quality and output

 - Advanced skills in After Effects and Figma

 - Comfortable in Premiere for editing

Bonus:

 - Rive, Lottie, GSAP, or web motion

 - 3D tools such as Cinema 4D or Blender

 - AI video tools

 - Experience building reusable motion systems or libraries




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team. We work together because it helps us move faster, collaborate closely, and produce better work. You should expect to be in the office as your default in your chosen hub.

This is a high trust environment. You’ll have real ownership, clear responsibility, and room to shape how things are done.

We care about pace, but also craft. The standard matters.

This role suits people who want responsibility, autonomy, and visible impact.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

 - Ownership of motion at one of Europe’s fastest-growing AI companies

 - Real budget

source_jobs.title: Account Executive - SMB NYC
source_jobs.description:
ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE - SMB NYC

New York




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring an SMB Account Executive to drive high-velocity sales across smaller and mid-sized legal teams.

This is a full-cycle role focused on running a high volume of opportunities, moving quickly from first conversation to close.

You’ll need to qualify hard, manage your time well, and keep deals moving. You’ll work closely with SDRs, Marketing, and Product, but you own the number.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Manage a high volume of inbound and outbound opportunities

 - Qualify quickly and focus on the deals that matter

 - Run efficient discovery and demos with a clear focus on value

 - Close deals in shorter sales cycles (typically 2–6 weeks)

 - Consistently hit and exceed revenue and activity targets

 - Maintain clean pipeline and strong follow-up across all deals

 - Use CRM as your operating system to manage volume effectively




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 1–3 years of SaaS sales experience (or strong SDR ready to step up)

 - Evidence of hitting targets in a high-velocity environment

 - Strong qualification skills — you know when to push and when to walk away

 - Clear, confident communicator

 - Highly organised and disciplined with pipeline management

 - High energy and resilience — you can handle volume without losing quality




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team. We work together because it helps us move faster, learn quickly, and improve as a group. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

This is a fast-paced environment. You’ll be managing multiple deals at once, and momentum matters.

The people who do well here stay organised, keep control of their pipeline, and don’t let deals stall.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

 - Equity: Stock options with real upside

 - Market: Strong demand and fast-moving buyers

 - Sales cycle: 2–6 weeks with high deal volume

 - Support: SDR, Marketing, and Product working closely with Sales

 - Progression: Clear path into Mid-Market AE




WHY WORDSMITH

We’re growing quickly, bringing on new customers, and expanding how legal teams use AI.

SMB is a key part of that growth, with fast feedback loops and strong conversion.

If you want to run a high volume pipeline, close deals quickly, and develop strong sales fundamentals, this is a great place to do it.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive - Mid Market NYC
source_jobs.description:
ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE - MID MARKET NYC

New York




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring an Account Executive to drive new business across in-house legal teams.

This is a full-cycle role. You’ll be responsible for building pipeline, running tight sales processes, and closing deals. You’ll work closely with SDRs, Marketing, and Product, but you own the outcome.

This is an early-stage environment. Things move fast, priorities shift, and there isn’t always a clear playbook. You’ll need to make decisions, stay close to customers, and keep momentum.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and close a strong pipeline of mid-market opportunities

 - Run high-quality discovery and demos that focus on real customer problems

 - Consistently hit and exceed revenue and pipeline targets

 - Build relationships with Legal Ops leaders, General Counsel, and Heads of Legal

 - Work closely with Product and Marketing to sharpen messaging and improve conversion

 - Maintain clean pipeline and accurate forecasts — your CRM is your operating system




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years of SaaS sales experience, ideally mid-market

 - Track record of hitting quota and closing deals (~$50k ACV)

 - Strong in discovery, demo, and value-led selling

 - Comfortable selling into complex organisations and senior stakeholders

 - High ownership — you take responsibility for outcomes, not just activity

 - Organised, consistent, and reliable with strong follow-through




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team in New York. We work together because it makes us faster, sharper, and more effective. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

This is a high performance environment. We set a high bar, move quickly, and expect people to take ownership of real problems.

There isn’t always a clear path. The people who do well here figure things out, stay close to the work, and keep pushing until they get to the right outcome.

This won’t suit everyone. If you’re looking for a highly structured environment or clear boundaries around pace, this likely isn’t the right fit.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

 - Equity: Stock options with real upside

 - Market: Strong demand and engaged buyers

 - Sales cycle: 3–6 months with a mix of inbound and outbound

 - Support: SDR, Marketing, and Product working closely with Sales

 - Progression: Clear path to Enterprise AE or Sales Leadership




WHY WORDSMITH

We’re in a period of strong momentum — growing quickly, winning new customers, and expanding into larger accounts.

The product is landing well, the market is ready, and the team is scaling to meet demand.

If you want to be in the room, close meaningful deals, and grow quickly in a high bar environment, this is one of the best places to do it.

source_jobs.title: GTM Legal Engineer
source_jobs.description:
GTM Legal Engineer

New York


Wordsmith

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.



The Role

In-house legal teams are navigating one of the biggest shifts in how corporate legal operates — from pressure to do more with less, to increasing demand for strategic input, to the arrival of AI that can genuinely change how work gets done. This role exists to be their guide through that shift.



You will come from a background in law with in-house experience who brings credibility to every stage of the commercial cycle. Working alongside Account Executives and GTM broadly, you will support engagements with General Counsels, Heads of Legal, Legal Operations leaders, and lawyers across inhouse departments — from first conversation through to deep team wide adoption.



This isn't a traditional sales support role. It combines legal ops expertise, solutions architecture, change advisory, and client partnership. Your mission is to help in-house legal teams understand what AI can genuinely do for them — and then make that real.



What You'll Do

Pre-Sale & GTM Support

 - Partner with Account Executives across the full sales cycle, bringing in-house credibility and knowledge to discovery, demos, and commercial conversations with legal leadership and team members.

 - Lead tailored product demonstrations for and in-house legal teams — grounding every demo in the operational realities of inhouse legal work.

 - Conduct in-depth discovery to understand how each legal department is structured, how work flows, where the inefficiencies live, and where Wordsmith can deliver the most meaningful impact, from contract management and regulatory compliance to matter management and external counsel oversight.

 - Hands on support during pilots or evaluation periods, helping to connect specific business needs with the developing technology.

 - Translate the value of AI into language that resonates with in-house stakeholders: reduced cycle times, cost efficiency, risk reduction, and freeing lawyers to focus on business priorities.

 - Confidently engage on ROI — helping legal and finance stakeholders evaluate and articulate the business case for AI adoption internally. Map how AI will impact specific functions and workflows.

 - Act as the voice of the in-house customer within the GTM team, feeding commercial and product teams with insight on how corporate legal departments think, prioritise, and buy.

Product Feedback

 - Be a leading voice for in-house users inside the company — sharing insights from legal ops teams, commercial lawyers, and compliance functions that directly inform product development and roadmap priorities.

 - Work closely with product and engineering teams as well as embedded legal engineers to translate real-world in-house legal needs into clear, actionable product requirements.

Market engagement

 - Help develop commercial messaging, content, and collateral that speaks directly to the priorities of GCs, Legal Ops leaders, and in-house teams.

 - Contribute to the development of the legal engineering functi

source_jobs.title: Legal Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
LEGAL SOLUTIONS ENGINEER

Edinburgh




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

Legal Solutions Engineers turn the product into real outcomes for customers.

You’ll work with customers after purchase to design, configure, and implement legal workflows inside Wordsmith. This role sits close to the customer, helping them embed the platform into how their legal function actually operates.

It’s part implementation, part problem solving, and part advisory. You’ll combine legal understanding with product knowledge to help customers get meaningful value from the platform.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Customer Implementation

 - Lead implementation projects for new customers

 - Design workflows and automation tailored to each organisation

 - Configure the platform to support real legal use cases

Solution Design

 - Work directly with legal teams and Legal Ops leaders to understand how they operate

 - Translate legal processes into structured workflows and AI-enabled automation

 - Advise on best practices for implementing legal technology

Customer Success & Expansion

 - Support onboarding and early adoption

 - Help customers expand into additional workflows and use cases

 - Partner with Account Management to identify growth opportunities

Product Feedback

 - Surface patterns, insights, and recurring requests to Product and Engineering

 - Help shape how the product evolves based on real usage




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience in legal tech, legal operations, or process transformation

 - Strong understanding of how in-house legal teams work

 - Ability to manage customer projects and guide stakeholders through change

 - Comfortable working across legal and technical domains

 - Structured thinker — you can turn messy processes into clear systems

 - Strong communication skills and confidence working directly with customers



Typical backgrounds include:

 - Legal Engineers

 - Legal Operations professionals

 - Lawyers who have moved into legal tech

 - Implementation or solutions consultants in SaaS with a legal background




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team in Edinburgh. We work together because it helps us collaborate closely across product, engineering, and customer teams. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

This is a high ownership role. You’ll be trusted to run projects, work directly with customers, and drive outcomes without heavy oversight.

The problems are often not fully defined. Part of the role is bringing structure and clarity to how legal work gets done.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

The value of legal technology comes from how it’s implemented.

Legal Solutions Engineers ensure customers successfully embed Wordsmith into their workflows and see real impact.

This role sits at the centre of that.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

 - Direct impact on customer outcomes and product adoption

 - Close collaboration with Product, Engineering, and GTM teams

 - A chance to shape how legal teams use AI in practice

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

source_jobs.title: Commercial Associate (SDR)
source_jobs.description:
COMMERCIAL ASSOCIATE (SDR)


NEW YORK



Wordsmith

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.



The Role

We’re hiring a Sales Development Representative (SDR) to spearhead our outbound engine and book high-quality opportunities with in-house legal teams.

This is the frontline of our growth. You’ll be responsible for finding the right accounts, crafting compelling outreach, and breaking into companies. You’ll work hand-in-hand with our Account Executives (AEs) and Marketing teams to build a predictable pipeline.

This is an early-stage environment. Things move fast, priorities shift, and there isn’t always a clear playbook. You’ll need to be resilient, highly organized, and willing to experiment with new ways to get a prospect’s attention.

What you’ll do

 - Build and execute targeted outbound campaigns (email, cold calling, LinkedIn) to general counsel and legal leaders.

 - Consistently hit and exceed monthly meeting-booked and pipeline targets.

 - Partner closely with AEs to strategize on key accounts and ensure smooth handoffs.

 - Act as a feedback loop for Marketing and Product by tracking what messaging resonates most with the market.

 - Maintain meticulous CRM data — keeping track of accounts, prospects, and outreach activity.

What we’re looking for

 - 1+ year of SaaS sales or lead generation experience (experience selling to legal, finance, or compliance is a major plus, but not required).

 - A relentless work ethic — you aren't afraid of the phone and view "no" as data, not a deterrent.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills; you can cut through the noise with clear, value-led messaging.

 - High curiosity and coachability; you want to master the product, the market, and the art of sales.

 - High ownership — you take responsibility for outcomes, not just logging activities.

 - Organized, consistent, and reliable with excellent time management.

How we work

 - We’re an in-office team in New York. We work together because it makes us faster, sharper, and more effective. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

 - This is a high-performance environment. We set a high bar, move quickly, and expect people to take ownership of real problems.

 - There isn’t always a clear path. The people who do well here figure things out, stay close to the work, and keep pushing until they get to the right outcome.

What you can expect

 - Equity: Stock options with real upside.

 - Market: Strong demand and engaged buyers facing real pain.

 - Training: Direct mentorship from sales leadership and close collaboration with AEs.

 - Progression: A clear, performance-based path to Account Executive and beyond.

Why Wordsmith

We’re in a period of strong momentum — growing quickly, winning new customers, and expanding into larger accounts.

The product is landing well, the market is ready, and the team is scaling to meet demand.

If you want to be in the room, master the fundamentals of tech sales, and grow quickly in a high-bar environment, this is one of the best places to do it.

source_jobs.title: Strategic Partner Manager (Ecosystem & Frontier Alliances)
source_jobs.description:
STRATEGIC PARTNER MANAGER (ECOSYSTEM & FRONTIER ALLIANCES)

London / New York (Hybrid)



Wordsmith

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.



The Role

Our product sits on the absolute frontier of AI. We work hand-in-hand with the foundation labs building that frontier - Anthropic and Microsoft. As they double down on ecosystems like Claude for Legal and Microsoft 365, our relationships with their teams directly dictate how fast we ship, how we scale product adoption, and how we win the market.

This role exists to own the day-to-day execution, strategic alignment, and GTM activation of these critical flagship relationships. You will act as the high-leverage interface connecting Wordsmith with our partner organisations, sitting at the intersection of Product, Engineering, and Marketing.

This isn't a traditional, reseller-focused BD seat. It is a sophisticated hybrid role requiring deep product empathy and sharp GTM execution. Your mission is to push deep product adoption, secure early access to frontier models, and co-ship the joint marketing moments that make Wordsmith the definitive legal application on top of their tech stacks.



What You'll Do

Product Collaboration & Deep Adoption

 - Bridge the Product Teams: Act as the primary interface between Wordsmith’s product/engineering org and our partners' applied AI, product, and developer-relations teams.

 - Drive Native Ecosystem Integration: Ensure Wordsmith is deeply embedded into our partners’ core surfaces (e.g., Anthropic’s MCP connector ecosystem, Claude for Legal, and Microsoft Copilot) so customers experience a seamless application stack.

 - Secure the Frontier Roadmap: Track partner release schedules to unlock early model previews, platform betas, and co-build opportunities for our engineering team.

 - Manage Technical Inbound: Own incoming partner opportunities (betas, credits, co-builds) and strategically route them internally to keep our platform ahead of the curve.

Co-Marketing & Ecosystem Growth

 - Ship High-Impact Launches: Partner with our marketing team and partners to execute joint case studies, technical blog posts, and major partner ecosystem feature launches.

 - Insert Wordsmith into the AI Narrative: Keep Wordsmith top-of-mind with partner marketing teams so we are featured unprompted in their customer reels, ecosystem showcases, and keynotes.

 - Convert Events to Pipeline: Project-manage our presence at a high-leverage joint event calendar (e.g., Anthropic Summits, Microsoft Build/Ignite), turning hallway conversations into product feedback and sales pipeline.

Relationship Management & Governance

 - Maintain Relationship Cadence: Run a crisp operational cadence with partner contacts while helping establish senior-to-senior touchpoints that our executive team can rely on.

 - Map the Ecosystem: Build and maintain an active map of stakeholders across our partners—from applied AI engineering leads to product marketing executives.



What We’re Looking For

Essential

 - 4–8+ years of experience in strategic partnerships, bu

source_jobs.title: Commercial Legal Counsel
source_jobs.description:
COMMERCIAL LEGAL COUNSEL

Edinburgh / London




ABOUT WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

This is not a traditional in-house counsel role. You are “customer zero” for an AI-native legal product.

You will support commercial agreements in a high-velocity SaaS environment while using Wordsmith daily to draft, review and improve how legal work gets done.

Alongside this, you will help build the systems behind the work. Playbooks, templates and workflows that allow the business to move faster without always relying on legal.

This role sits at the intersection of legal execution, product development and commercial velocity.




RESPONSIBILITIES


COMMERCIAL LEGAL SUPPORT

Provide fast, pragmatic legal support across customer and vendor agreements including MSAs, order forms, DPAs and NDAs.

Balance legal risk with commercial priorities to keep deals progressing.

Partner closely with Sales and GTM teams through active deal cycles, especially during end-of-month and quarter close.




AI & LEGAL WORKFLOW DEVELOPMENT

Use AI tools to draft, review and negotiate contracts efficiently.

Build and refine templates, playbooks and self-serve workflows that scale legal output.

Identify opportunities to automate or remove friction in the contracting process.




CUSTOMER ZERO & PRODUCT FEEDBACK

Act as an internal user of Wordsmith. Test, break and improve the product through real legal work.

Work with Product and Legal Engineering to translate legal workflows into product features.

Feed structured insights back into R&D to improve accuracy, usability and impact.




LEGAL OPERATIONS & INSIGHT

Analyse contract data to identify trends, improve positions and strengthen playbooks.

Bring a data-led approach to how legal decisions are made and scaled.




REGULATORY & RISK

Provide practical advice on privacy, data protection and SaaS contracting.

Stay current on GDPR, AI regulation and emerging legal frameworks.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Qualified solicitor (England & Wales or Scotland) with roughly 2–4 years’ PQE.

 - Strong grounding in B2B SaaS agreements and commercial contracting.

 - Experience working closely with sales teams or commercial stakeholders.

 - Practical understanding of privacy and data protection.

 - Comfort operating in a fast-moving environment where speed and judgement both matter.

 - Ability to explain legal concepts clearly and make decisions without over-engineering.

 - Interest in AI, legal technology and improving how legal work gets done.




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team. We work together because it makes us faster, sharper, and more effective. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

This is a high performance environment. We set a high bar, move quickly, and expect people to take ownership of real problems.

There isn’t always a clear path. The people who do well here figure things out, stay close to the work, and keep pushing until they get to the right outcome.

This won’t suit everyone. If you’re looking for a highly struc

source_jobs.title: Partner Manager
source_jobs.description:
Partner Manager

London



Wordsmith

Wordsmith is building the AI-enabled command centre for in-house legal teams.

Our SI and consulting partners play a critical role in helping customers implement, customise, and get the most value out of the platform.

We're looking for someone to build and scale the partner relationships that make that possible.

The Role

Partner Managers develop and manage Wordsmith's strategic referral partners.

Based from our London office, you'll build deep, commercially impactful relationships with SI and consulting partners who support our customers through implementation, delivery, and ongoing adoption of the Wordsmith AI platform. You'll also manage a number of strategic ISV referral partners.

You'll drive co-sell motions, identify joint account opportunities, and make sure partners are fully enabled to deliver great customer outcomes. This is a highly relationship-driven role that sits at the heart of our go-to-market strategy.



What You'll Do

Co-Sell Strategy & Account Planning

 - Develop and execute co-sell motions with SI and consulting partners, identifying joint target accounts and driving coordinated sales efforts to accelerate pipeline and close deals.

 - Run regular account mapping sessions with partners to identify overlapping opportunities, whitespace, and mutual accounts for joint pursuit.

 - Own partner-influenced and partner-sourced pipeline reporting, tracking co-sell contribution to quarterly and annual revenue targets.

Partner Enablement & Delivery

 - Ensure SI partners are fully trained and certified on the Wordsmith AI platform, equipping them to lead implementation, customisation, and delivery engagements.

 - Work closely with partners to ensure high-quality implementation and delivery for joint customers, acting as an escalation point and quality assurance layer.

Relationship Management & Recruitment

 - Build and maintain strong executive and practitioner-level relationships across SI partner organisations, conducting regular business reviews and strategic planning sessions.

 - Identify, evaluate, and onboard new SI and consulting partners that align with our target market segments and customer needs.

Commercial Governance

 - Manage partner agreements, deal registration, and commercial frameworks to ensure clear rules of engagement and mutual accountability.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Work closely with Sales, Customer Success, and Product teams to align on partner-driven opportunities and ensure seamless customer experiences.
   
   

What we're looking for

Essential

 - 3–6 years of experience in channel partnerships, SI/consulting alliances, or partner management within a SaaS or enterprise technology company.

 - Proven track record of driving co-sell motions and generating partner-influenced pipeline and revenue.

 - Strong experience with account mapping, joint account planning, and coordinated selling with partners.

 - Deep understanding of the SI and consulting partner landscape, including how professional services firms operate and make decisions.

 - Excellent relationship management skills, with the ability to build trust and credibility at both executive and practitioner levels.

 - Commercial negotiation skills, with experience managing partner agreements and deal registration processes.

 - Strong communication and presentation skills, comfortable leading QBRs, partner enablement sessions, and executive briefings.

 - Self-motivated, organised, and able to manage multiple partner relationships and priorities simultaneously.

Valued

 - Experience working in or with the legal technology or legal services industry.

 - Existing relationships with consulting firms, legal operations consultancies, or Big Four/mid-tier advisory practices.

 - Familiarity with partner relationship management tools (e.g. Crossbeam, Reveal, Salesforce PRM).

 - Understanding of AI/ML products and how they're implemented in enterprise environments.



Why this r

source_jobs.title: Tech Recruiter (R&D)
source_jobs.description:
TECHNICAL RECRUITER (R&D)

Edinburgh




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Technical Recruiter to support hiring across our R&D organisation.

This includes Product Engineers, AI/LLM Engineers, Product Managers, and Designers. You’ll work across disciplines, partnering with leaders to build balanced, high-performing teams.

This is a hands-on role. You’ll need strong sourcing, good judgement across different roles, and the ability to run high-quality processes end to end.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own hiring across engineering, product, and design

 - Build strong pipelines through proactive sourcing across the UK and Europe

 - Identify and engage high-quality candidates across multiple disciplines

 - Partner with engineering, product, and design leaders to define roles and hiring plans

 - Run end-to-end hiring processes, from role kick-off to offer

 - Maintain a high bar on candidate quality and experience

 - Improve hiring playbooks, interview design, and recruiting systems as we scale




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience hiring across engineering and at least one of product or design

 - Strong sourcing ability and confidence engaging passive candidates

 - Good judgement on different role types — you understand what strong looks like across disciplines

 - Ability to partner with technical and product stakeholders

 - High ownership and consistency — you keep processes moving and close roles

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-moving, evolving environment

 - Based in Edinburgh or willing to relocate




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team in Edinburgh. We work together because it helps us collaborate closely and make better hiring decisions. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

This is a high ownership role. You won’t be handed perfect briefs or fully built processes. Part of the role is helping shape both.

We care about quality. The goal is not just to fill roles, but to build strong, balanced teams across engineering, product, and design.

This won’t suit someone who prefers a narrow or purely process-driven role.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

The teams we build now will define the product for years to come.

Hiring across engineering, product, and design well is one of the hardest things to get right. This role sits at the centre of that.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

 - Close partnership with engineering, product, and design leadership

 - A strong product and engineering culture focused on ownership and shipping

 - A role where recruiting is treated as a core function, not support

source_jobs.title: Account Executive - Mid Market EMEA
source_jobs.description:
ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE - MID MARKET EMEA


Remote EU - Amsterdam / Munich / Barcelona

Languages (Any of) - Dutch, German, Spanish, French




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring an Account Executive to drive new business across in-house legal teams.

This is a full-cycle role. You’ll be responsible for building pipeline, running tight sales processes, and closing deals. You’ll work closely with SDRs, Marketing, and Product, but you own the outcome.

This is an early-stage environment. Things move fast, priorities shift, and there isn’t always a clear playbook. You’ll need to make decisions, stay close to customers, and keep momentum.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and close a strong pipeline of mid-market opportunities

 - Run high-quality discovery and demos that focus on real customer problems

 - Consistently hit and exceed revenue and pipeline targets

 - Build relationships with Legal Ops leaders, General Counsel, and Heads of Legal

 - Work closely with Product and Marketing to sharpen messaging and improve conversion

 - Maintain clean pipeline and accurate forecasts — your CRM is your operating system




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years of SaaS sales experience, ideally mid-market

 - Track record of hitting quota and closing deals (~$50k ACV)

 - Strong in discovery, demo, and value-led selling

 - Comfortable selling into complex organisations and senior stakeholders

 - High ownership — you take responsibility for outcomes, not just activity

 - Organised, consistent, and reliable with strong follow-through




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team in New York. We work together because it makes us faster, sharper, and more effective. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

This is a high performance environment. We set a high bar, move quickly, and expect people to take ownership of real problems.

There isn’t always a clear path. The people who do well here figure things out, stay close to the work, and keep pushing until they get to the right outcome.

This won’t suit everyone. If you’re looking for a highly structured environment or clear boundaries around pace, this likely isn’t the right fit.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

 - Equity: Stock options with real upside

 - Market: Strong demand and engaged buyers

 - Sales cycle: 3–6 months with a mix of inbound and outbound

 - Support: SDR, Marketing, and Product working closely with Sales

 - Progression: Clear path to Enterprise AE or Sales Leadership




WHY WORDSMITH

We’re in a period of strong momentum — growing quickly, winning new customers, and expanding into larger accounts.

The product is landing well, the market is ready, and the team is scaling to meet demand.

If you want to be in the room, close meaningful deals, and grow quickly in a high bar environment, this is one of the best places to do it.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive - Enterprise NYC
source_jobs.description:

New York




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.

WHAT YOU’LL DO

HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team in New York. We work together because it makes us faster, sharper, and more effective. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

This is a high performance environment. We set a high bar, move quickly, and expect people to take ownership of real problems.

There isn’t always a clear path. The people who do well here figure things out, stay close to the work, and keep pushing until they get to the right outcome.

WHY WORDSMITH

source_jobs.title: IT Security & Compliance Lead
source_jobs.description:
I.T Security, Privacy & AI Governance Lead

Edinburgh

Wordsmith

Wordsmith is building the AI-enabled command centre for in-house legal teams.

Our customers are some of the most demanding enterprise legal departments in the world, and they hold us to a high bar on how we secure our systems, devices, and infrastructure.

We're looking for someone to build the IT security function that keeps that trust intact as we scale.

The Role

Security, Privacy & AI Governance Leads make sure Wordsmith's systems, devices, and infrastructure stay secure as the company grows.

You'll build our IT security function from the ground up — covering device management, identity and access, infrastructure controls, and incident response — while also owning the compliance and AI governance work that keeps enterprise customers confident in how we operate.

It's a hands-on, build-it-yourself role. You'll choose the tooling, set the controls, and make sure security is baked into how the company operates day to day, not bolted on afterwards.

What You'll Do

IT Security & Device Management

 - Own device compliance and mobile device management (MDM) across the company, using tools like Jamf or Intune to keep endpoints secure and compliant.

 - Manage identity and access management (IAM) — provisioning, access reviews, and least-privilege controls — using tools like Okta.

 - Own infrastructure and cloud security controls across our environment (e.g. AWS), working closely with Engineering to keep systems hardened.

Security Operations & Incident Response

 - Lead security incident response — full lifecycle investigations, coordinating with internal teams and external partners (e.g. SOCaaS providers), and running post-incident reviews and tabletop exercises.

 - Operate and tune security monitoring and detection tooling (EDR, DLP, SIEM, or similar) to catch and respond to threats quickly.

Compliance & Certification

 - Own SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001/27017/27018 end-to-end, from policy design through to audit evidence and the audits themselves.

 - Automate evidence collection to cut audit overhead and keep the program running without heavy manual effort.

AI Governance & Vendor Risk

 - Assess third-party vendors and AI tools for security and privacy risk before they're adopted, and put the right safeguards in place.

 - Support Wordsmith's AI governance program, including risk reviews tied to how AI is used across the product.

Customer & Deal Support

 - Partner with Sales, Customer Success, and Legal to support enterprise deals — security questionnaires, DPAs, and contract terms — without slowing the business down.

 - Manage our Trust Center, giving customers self-service access to our security and compliance documentation.

What we're looking for

Essential

 - Experience running IT security operations at a fast-growing SaaS company, including device management and identity and access management.

 - Hands-on experience with MDM platforms such as Jamf or Intune.

 - Hands-on experience with IAM tooling (e.g. Okta) and cloud infrastructure security (e.g. AWS).

 - Experience leading security incident response, from investigation through to post-incident review.

 - Working knowledge of SOC 2 and the ISO 27000 series.

 - Comfortable evaluating and operating security tooling such as EDR, DLP, or SIEM platforms.

 - A strong cross-functional operator, comfortable bridging IT, Security, Engineering, and GTM teams.

Valued

 - Exposure to privacy regulation (e.g. GDPR) and AI governance frameworks (e.g. ISO 42001).

 - Relevant certifications — e.g. CISSP/ISC2, CCSK, CIPP/E, or AIGP.

 - Experience in legal tech, AI, or another highly regulated SaaS environment.

 - Familiarity with tools such as Vanta, Crowdstrike, Zscaler, Datadog, or Whistic.

Why this role matters

The trust enterprise legal teams place in us depends on how well we secure our systems and devices — this role is central to that.

Your work will directly shape how the company handles

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT ENGINEER

Edinburgh




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT

Our engineering team is not using AI as a feature. It’s how we build.

Engineers here are expected to explore, test, and apply AI directly in how we design and ship product. There’s no heavy process, no layers of approval, and no hesitation around new approaches.

If something works, we use it. If it doesn’t, we move on quickly.

You’ll have the freedom to experiment, shape how AI is used in the product, and push beyond the patterns most teams are still stuck in.




THE ROLE

You’ll build product features end to end, working across frontend and backend, and ship AI-native workflows used by real customers.

You’ll work closely with design, product, and legal engineers to turn complex problems into simple, high-quality user experiences.

This is a role for someone who wants to move fast, stay close to users, and have a real impact on what gets built.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own features end to end across frontend and backend

 - Build AI-native workflows using LLMs and structured data

 - Collaborate closely with design, product, and legal engineering

 - Ship quickly, learn from users, and iterate

 - Contribute to architecture, tooling, and engineering practices

 - Help define how we use AI across the product




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years building high-quality web products

 - Strong experience with modern frontend tools (React, Next.js, TypeScript)

 - Backend experience (Python, Node, AWS or similar)

 - Strong product instincts — you care about UX, clarity, and outcomes

 - Comfortable working in fast-moving environments with evolving systems

 - Interest in AI and how it changes how software is built

Bonus:

 - Experience with LLMs, prompt design, or AI workflows

 - Background in building internal tools or complex workflows




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team in Edinburgh. We work together because it helps us build faster, collaborate closely, and make better product decisions. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

This is a high trust environment. Engineers have real ownership over what they build and how they build it.

We keep process light and focus on shipping, learning, and improving quickly.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

 - Real ownership over product and technical decisions

 - The chance to work at the frontier of AI-native product development

 - A team of engineers, designers, and domain experts working closely together

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity




WHY WORDSMITH

We’re growing quickly, shipping fast, and solving real problems for customers.

The product is evolving rapidly, and engineering plays a central role in how far and how fast we can go.

If you want to build with AI as a core part of your workflow, not just a layer on top, this is a strong place to do it.

source_jobs.title: GTM Recruiter
source_jobs.description:

New York




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

THE ROLE

We’re hiring a GTM Recruiter to build the team that takes our product to market.

This is not a coordination role. You’ll own hiring across Sales, Marketing, Partnerships, and Customer Success in the US.

You’ll work directly with commercial leadership, shape hiring plans, and drive processes end to end. You’ll be expected to push on briefs, raise the bar, and move quickly.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own GTM hiring across the US

 - Hire across roles including AEs, SDRs, Sales Engineers, Customer Success, Partnerships, and Marketing

 - Partner closely with commercial leaders to define roles, structure teams, and align on hiring plans

 - Build strong pipelines through proactive sourcing and outreach

 - Run high-quality end-to-end processes, from kick-off to offer

 - Maintain a high bar on candidate quality and experience

 - Improve hiring playbooks, interview design, and overall recruiting systems as we scale




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience hiring GTM roles in high-growth B2B SaaS environments

 - Strong track record placing high-quality commercial talent

 - Good judgement on talent — you know what “great” looks like and hold the line

 - Comfortable challenging hiring managers and shaping briefs

 - High ownership — you take responsibility for outcomes, not just activity

 - Able to operate at pace and manage multiple roles without losing quality

 - Based in New York




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team in New York. We work together because it helps us move faster, make better decisions, and stay aligned. You should expect to be in the office as your default.

This is a high ownership environment. You won’t be handed perfect briefs or fully built processes. Part of the role is shaping both.

We move quickly and expect a high standard. The people who do well here are proactive, direct, and focused on outcomes.

This won’t suit someone looking for a narrow execution role or a highly structured environment.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

 - Direct access to commercial leadership and real influence on how teams are built

 - Ownership of a critical function as we scale in the US

 - A fast-moving environment with clear impact from your work

 - Competitive compensation and meaningful equity




WHY WORDSMITH

We’re building in a market with real demand, strong momentum, and clear product value.

Hiring is a core lever for how we scale, and this role sits at the centre of that.

If you want to move beyond running processes and actually shape a commercial organisation, this is a strong place to do it.

source_jobs.title: Designer, Web & Brand
source_jobs.description:
DESIGNER, WEB & BRAND

Full-time · Edinburgh/London



Wordsmith is building the AI platform for in-house legal teams. We take the work that used to mean expensive outside counsel and endless email threads and turn it into something fast, in-house, and genuinely good to use.



We just closed our Series B. That means more people, more product, and a lot more brand to build. Which is where you come in.



We're looking for a designer with serious visual taste to lead our work across web and brand. Not someone who waits for a brief and colors it in, but someone who can pick up an ambiguous problem and turn it into something sharp. For us it's both how it works and how it looks, down to the last pixel and the last full stop. We care about craft the way good lawyers care about commas.



This role sits at the center of how Wordsmith looks and sounds to the world: the website, the campaigns, the brand system, the events we show up at, and the small details that make people stop and pay attention.




LOCATION AND WORK MODE

This role is based in Edinburgh or London. We like being in the same room for the messy, generative parts of the work, and we trust people to do focused work wherever they do it best.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and build the website and landing pages, working hand in hand with engineers

 - Own and evolve the Wordsmith brand system across every surface it lives on

 - Partner with marketing and copy to turn ideas into campaigns that carry across channels

 - Bring the brand into the real world, designing everything from our stand and collateral at events to merchandise and the details people remember on the way out

 - Sweat the motion, the interaction, and the detail until each piece feels inevitable




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - A body of work that speaks louder than a CV. We care about what you've made, not where you studied

 - Excellent visual, UI, motion, and interactive design skills

 - An instinct for taste and judgment. Knowing what to cut, what to keep, and when something is not good enough yet

 - A bias for action. You see what needs doing and start, without waiting for a brief or permission

 - Comfort working in the open, shipping fast, and iterating with engineers in real time

 - A genuine love for the craft, and an allergy to the generic




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

 - The chance to define a brand at the moment it scales, not maintain one that's already set

 - A small, opinionated team that cares deeply about the work and the people doing it

 - Modern tooling and the autonomy to use it well

source_jobs.title: 🚀 Don't see your perfect role? Join our Talent Pool!
source_jobs.description:
🚀 DON'T SEE YOUR PERFECT ROLE? JOIN OUR TALENT POOL!



At Wordsmith AI, we are constantly pushing the boundaries of technology and innovation. Just because we don't have an open position that fits your exact toolkit today, doesn't mean we won't need your superpowers tomorrow.



If you are passionate about AI, love solving complex problems, and want to help shape the future Legal, we want to hear from you.



We review our speculative applications regularly. If a potential match pops up, our talent acquisition team will be in touch!

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT DESIGNER

Edinburgh




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

We're looking for someone who lives at the intersection of design and code. You'll work directly with product and engineering to frame the problem, design the flows, then prototype, build, and polish the experiences that make Wordsmith feel effortless, even when the workflows underneath are anything but. This role suits someone who thinks like a designer and builds like an engineer, or the other way round.

You'll design products where the behaviour is not always fixed, the inputs are messy, and the outcome still has to feel simple and reliable. You'll own that outcome end to end, and you'll be trusted to run with it on your own.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and build the UX and UI: the flows, components, and interactions that simplify complex legal workflows

 - Rapidly prototype new concepts in code to validate ideas before committing to a full build

 - Bridge design and engineering: turn designs into shipped code, and push design forward through technical craft

 - Own the quality of UI implementation across the product, from pixel-perfect execution to the small interactions that make it feel alive

 - Help define product direction, frame ambiguous problems, and design the end-to-end flows behind the interface

 - Contribute to the design system and component libraries that let the team ship faster

 - Use AI as part of how you work, to explore, prototype, and think past traditional UX and UI patterns




YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF

 - You can shape the direction of projects and bring people with you. You've earned the trust of engineers, PMs, and senior stakeholders before, and you know how to move a team without holding a mandate

 - You can build and ship your own front-end (TypeScript, React, CSS, animation). AI writes a lot of it now, so the real bar is taste and judgment: knowing what good looks like and getting it the last 10%

 - You're self-sufficient: you can take a problem from ambiguity to shipped experience on your own, no hand-holding required

 - You have an eye for detail and care deeply about craft, in the flows and logic as much as the pixels

 - You finish what you start: polished and shipped, not left at good enough

 - You move fast, prototyping ideas in hours, not days

 - You think in systems and build reusable, maintainable UI

 - You're excited about AI and want to help define new interaction paradigms for an entire industry




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience building or scaling design systems

 - Experience building developer tools, technical products, or other complex software for expert users

 - Formal design training, or a track record that makes it irrelevant

 - A real interest in how legal work actually gets done




HOW WE WORK

We're an in-office team in Edinburgh. We work together because it helps us collaborate closely, move faster, and make better product decisions. Expect the office as your default. Design sits tightly with product and engineering, so you'll be in the room for

source_jobs.title: Account Executive - Enterprise EMEA
source_jobs.description:
ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE - ENTERPRISE EMEA



Remote EU - Amsterdam / Munich / Barcelona

Languages (Any of) - Dutch, German, Spanish, French




WORDSMITH

Wordsmith is building the legal operations platform for in-house teams, and we are growing fast.

We believe in-house legal is becoming one of the most important functions in the AI era. Every business is moving faster, but legal work is still too often scattered across inboxes, chats, documents, and disconnected systems.

Wordsmith brings that work into one system. Requests come in from across the business, AI agents handle the routine, lawyers stay in control where judgment matters, and every step is captured as work happens.

We are built for in-house legal, not law firms. Our goal is to help legal teams move faster, reduce unnecessary external counsel spend, and become a function the business can run with.

More than 500 companies, including Financial Times, Safelite, Trip.com, Canva, Starling, and Sage, use Wordsmith. Backed by $100M from Index Ventures, General Catalyst, Highland Europe, and others, we are building one of the defining companies in legal AI.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring an Enterprise Account Executive to lead complex, high-value deals across large organisations.

You’ll be responsible for building and progressing a focused set of strategic opportunities. This means navigating multiple stakeholders, building internal champions, and creating alignment across legal, technical, and commercial teams.

You’ll work closely with Legal Engineers, Product, and Leadership, but you own the deal strategy and the outcome.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own a portfolio of high-value, enterprise opportunities

 - Build and execute deal strategies across complex buying groups

 - Navigate stakeholders across Legal, Finance, Security, IT, and Procurement

 - Develop strong champions and drive internal alignment within accounts

 - Lead longer sales cycles (6–12+ months) with clear structure and momentum

 - Partner closely with GTM Legal Engineers on solution design

 - Maintain accurate forecasting and clear deal visibility




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience closing complex enterprise SaaS deals

 - Track record of landing and expanding high-value accounts

 - Strong stakeholder management across multiple functions

 - Ability to build champions and navigate internal politics

 - Structured, strategic approach to deal management

 - High ownership and accountability for outcomes




HOW WE WORK

We’re an in-office team. We work together because it makes us faster, sharper, and more effective. You should expect to be in the office our out visiting clients as your default.

This is a high performance environment. We set a high bar, move quickly, and expect people to take ownership of real problems.

There isn’t always a clear path. The people who do well here figure things out, stay close to the work, and keep pushing until they get to the right outcome.

This role requires patience, structure, and strong judgement. You’ll be trusted to run complex deals with a high degree of autonomy.




WHY WORDSMITH

We’re in a period of strong momentum — growing quickly, winning new customers, and expanding into larger accounts.

The product is landing well, the market is ready, and the team is scaling to meet demand.

If you want to be in the room, close meaningful deals, and grow quickly in a high bar environment, this is one of the best places to do it.

notes: Wordsmith is a legal operations platform with a mix of tech and non-tech roles. The company has a strong engineering core, but also has a significant number of GTM roles.
